items.title: Enhance VideoProvider with Midjourney in S D K 🎥
items.summary: This update adds 'midjourney' to the VideoProvider type in both TypeScript and Python SDKs, enabling access to the new `/midjourney/videos` endpoint. The client.video provider tables in the READMEs have also been updated to reflect this addition. Users can now generate video tasks using Midjourney seamlessly.

items.title: Enhance Microsoft OAuth Integration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update wires the Microsoft OAuth K8s secret, enabling the auth-backend to read necessary environment variables for proper configuration. Additionally, it expands the scope options for Microsoft connectors, allowing users to opt for read/write permissions during installation. These changes improve the overall functionality and user experience when integrating Microsoft services.

items.title: Streamline CNAME Flow and Simplify Domain Management in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the domain management process by collapsing the SiteDomain schema to a single CNAME instruction, enhancing user experience. The changes include dropping the EdgeOne wizard and streamlining the verification process, which now uniformly offers a 'Verify Now' action for all non-active rows. Additionally, internationalization files have been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring consistency across languages.

items.title: Fix input clipping issue in Nexior 🛠️
items.summary: This update addresses a clipping issue with input focus rings in the Midjourney tab of the Nexior app. By insetting the tab-pane content by 4px, the focus glow now displays correctly without visual regression across all inputs. The change ensures that the focus rings are fully visible while maintaining the integrity of the layout.

items.title: Enhance Video Tab UX with Required Image Indicator in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces two key UX improvements for the Midjourney video generation feature. A red asterisk has been added to the first-frame image label to indicate that an image is required, and a client-side check now prompts users with a message if they attempt to generate a video without uploading an image. These changes aim to reduce user errors and improve the overall experience.

items.title: Fix video pricing display and clarify image requirements in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the video pricing display, ensuring accurate costs based on selected modes and resolutions. Additionally, it clarifies the requirement for an image URL when generating videos, preventing unnecessary errors for users. The changes enhance user experience and align the API documentation with actual constraints.

items.title: Enhance dark mode color consistency in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update modifies the SCSS for dark mode to ensure proper color rendering by deriving light and dark color variables from a single source of truth. The changes prevent light-on-light text issues in dark mode, enhancing visual accessibility. The light mode remains visually unchanged, ensuring a seamless experience across themes.

items.title: Enhance Dark Mode Visibility for Installed Cards in Frontend Web 🌙
items.summary: This update addresses visibility issues for installed skill cards in dark mode on the user skills page. The fix involves redefining color variables to ensure proper contrast, replacing near-white backgrounds with darker shades. As a result, text within installed cards is now clearly visible, improving user experience across multiple components.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automated translation updates for internationalization (i18n) based on changes from the zh-CN source. All relevant cache files have been renamed to reflect these updates, ensuring that the application remains up-to-date with the latest translations. This enhancement will improve the user experience for Chinese-speaking users.

items.title: Enhance health check for kubelet in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the tenant-proxy configuration to include a dedicated `/healthz` endpoint that bypasses the HTTP to HTTPS redirect. The readiness probe is now pointed at this new endpoint, ensuring the kubelet can successfully check the pod's health without encountering TLS issues. This change stabilizes the deployment process and improves pod readiness in the live cluster.

items.title: Integrate Microsoft 365 Connectors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new OAuth provider for Microsoft 365, enabling integration with OneDrive, Outlook Mail, and Outlook Calendar. Users can now access these services through a unified Microsoft account picker, enhancing the connector experience. Additionally, new environment variables and catalog entries have been added to support this functionality.

items.title: Update Connector URLs for Streamable HTTP in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update migrates the public connectors for Linear, Atlassian, and Asana from legacy SSE endpoints to the new Streamable HTTP endpoints. A migration script ensures existing user connections are updated without requiring re-authentication. The change enhances the reliability of the Browse Connectors UI by ensuring all connectors are using the correct transport method.

items.title: Enhance Image Processing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses two critical bugs affecting image-driven flows in the Nano Banana MCP server. The first fix ensures that the `image_url` is recognized as a fetchable string, allowing the model to utilize it correctly without prompting the user for a re-upload. The second fix enhances the pre-load mechanism to include slugs from previously loaded MCP servers, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Enhance Chat with Inline Source-Citation Chips in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces inline source-citation chips in the chat UI, allowing users to see citations as small numbered chips with hover preview cards. Each chip provides a quick view of the source label, title, snippet, and URL, enhancing the user experience by making it easier to reference information. The implementation ensures backward compatibility and maintains the integrity of existing chat functionalities.

items.title: Enhance Aichat2 with Inline Source Citations in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces inline source citation tags (`<acite>`) to Aichat2 conversations, allowing users to see references as superscript chips that link to original sources. The new feature enhances the user experience by providing provenance for external references, making it easier to track information sources. Additionally, the parser has been generalized to handle both `<acard>` and `<acite>` tags, ensuring seamless integration and improved functionality.

items.title: Introducing Caddy Tenant-Proxy for On-Demand TLS in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Caddy tenant-proxy, enabling on-demand Let's Encrypt TLS for tenant custom domains. It includes a new deployment configuration and service setup, ensuring that SSL certificates can be issued automatically upon request. The architecture supports up to 50,000 tenants with persistent storage for certificates, enhancing security and ease of use.

items.title: Fix skill source URLs to prevent 404s in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where skill source URLs were incorrectly generated, leading to 404 errors when accessing certain skills. The fix includes adding the skill root prefix back into the URL construction and addressing a deduplication bug for multi-root sources. As a result, users will now be able to view the correct source links for all skills in the Browse Skills dialog.

items.title: Refine Badge Logic for BYO MCP Connectors in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update modifies the badge display logic for connectors on the Connections page. Now, only genuinely user-authored MCP connectors will receive the 'Custom' badge, while catalog-curated connectors like Stripe and others will not, enhancing clarity for users. The change involves a single file modification to better differentiate between user-created and vendor-installed integrations.

items.title: Add public ask URL for Caddy on-demand TLS in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new public endpoint for the Caddy on-demand TLS feature, allowing it to validate tenant custom domains before issuing Let's Encrypt certificates. The endpoint is designed to be anonymous and does not affect existing functionality, ensuring a seamless transition as we migrate away from EdgeOne. This change is purely additive and sets the stage for future enhancements related to domain management.

items.title: Enhance Video Playback with Iframes in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update modifies the video card rendering in the chat component to use iframes instead of HTML5 video elements. This change ensures that YouTube URLs play correctly, addressing a previous issue where users encountered a black screen. The new implementation maintains compatibility with direct media URLs while providing a consistent user experience.

items.title: Fix YouTube Card Type to Video in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update corrects the handling of YouTube cards by ensuring the type remains 'video' instead of flipping to 'embed'. The change simplifies the frontend rendering process, allowing all video cards to be displayed using iframes without requiring a separate embed type. All tests have been updated and passed successfully, ensuring compatibility with previous card types.

items.title: Display Original Filenames in Chat Bubbles in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update enhances the chat experience by displaying the original filenames instead of opaque CDN URLs in message bubbles. Key changes include the introduction of a new computed property in the Composer component to track filenames and updates to the Conversation component to ensure these names are preserved when messages are sent. This improvement allows users to easily identify files in their chat history.

items.title: Enhance YouTube Video Playback with Embed URLs in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update rewrites YouTube URLs to use the embed format, allowing videos to play correctly in the assistant. The changes include normalizing various YouTube URL formats and updating the card type to 'embed' for proper rendering. Extensive testing confirms that all scenarios are covered, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Enhance Skills Browsing with Pagination and Publisher Dropdown in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces pagination to the Browse Skills dialog, reducing the displayed skills per page to 12 for improved usability. Additionally, the publisher dropdown now shows all available publishers regardless of the current page, ensuring users can filter effectively. These changes enhance the overall user experience by making navigation and filtering more intuitive.

items.title: Enhance Skill Catalog with 4 New Sources in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds four high-star, MIT-licensed Skill repositories to the default sources, expanding the total installable skills from approximately 25 to 233. The new sources include popular repositories like 'superpowers', 'mattpocock', 'scientific', and 'marketing', which collectively cover a broader range of skills. The integration is seamless, with automatic updates through the existing sync job, ensuring users have access to a richer skill set.

items.title: Enhance Chat Image Loading and Caption Style in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a loading placeholder for images in the chat component, preventing a broken-image flash during loading. Additionally, the caption style has been refined for better aesthetics, featuring centered alignment and a softer color scheme to enhance the visual appeal without overpowering the image.

items.title: Enhance chat experience by persisting composer drafts in Nexior ✨
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature that allows users to retain their unsubmitted composer drafts when switching between chat groups. By implementing an in-memory store for the draft, users can seamlessly transition between conversations without losing their typed content. This enhancement improves usability, especially for users exploring different models or deep-linking through connectors.

items.title: Update default suggestion model_group to ChatGPT in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the default suggestion model_group from Claude to ChatGPT in both the sync_connector_catalog and ConnectorCatalogItemSerializer. This ensures that users are directed to the expected ChatGPT model when deep-linking from the AuthFrontend, enhancing the user experience. Overrides in the manifest for specific suggestions remain intact.

items.title: Ensure DB Migrations Run Before Traffic in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an initContainer to the AuthBackend deployment, ensuring that database migrations are applied before the main pod starts serving traffic. This change addresses a critical issue where a missing column caused 500 errors in production. The initContainer reuses the main container's environment variables, streamlining the deployment process and preventing future migration-related errors.

items.title: Enhance Boot Screen Theme and Branding in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the Nexior boot screen by addressing two key issues: the white flash for dark-mode users and the lack of brand identity. It implements a pre-paint inline script to resolve the user's theme preference before the first paint, ensuring a seamless experience. Additionally, the boot screen now features the 'Ace Data Cloud' branding, enhancing the overall visual appeal.

items.title: Streamline Subsite Management and Enhance BYOK in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates subsite management directly into the user-settings dialog, improving user experience by eliminating the standalone subsite page. It introduces a one-click 'Build same site' feature on the About tab for the main site, while also scoping BYOK API keys per site using the x-site-origin header. These changes enhance usability and security for users managing multiple subsites.

items.title: Clean up Kling sidebar by removing disabled Avatar tab in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update removes the permanently disabled '数字人' (Avatar) tab from the Kling sidebar, streamlining the user interface. The change affects the TabSwitcher component and updates localization files to eliminate orphaned keys. Now, users will only see the actionable '视频生成' (Video Generation) and '动作控制' (Motion Control) tabs, enhancing usability.

items.title: Enhance Connector Descriptions for Better Localization in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the `acedata/*` connector catalog seeds by translating and rewriting descriptions to better serve zh-CN users. Key changes include enhanced `short_description` and `description` fields that now feature brand keywords and detailed capability lists, optimizing them for keyword matching. This ensures that on-demand MCP loaders can accurately route to the right tools, enhancing user experience across all 15 official connectors.

items.title: Fix catalog icons to CDN for seamless access in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update rehosts all connector icons on the CDN at cdn.acedata.cloud, ensuring reliable access and preventing broken images in the Browse Connectors dialog. The changes include updates to both community and partner rows in the connectors YAML files, with all URLs verified to return the correct image MIME types. This enhancement improves the overall user experience by eliminating potential failures due to third-party favicon links.

items.title: Elapsed Time Now Visible on Task Previews in Nexior ⏱️
items.summary: This update introduces an 'Elapsed' time display on task preview cards across 15 generation scenarios, allowing users to see how long each task took without needing to access DevTools or logs. The elapsed time is now included in the task summary, enhancing user experience and providing immediate feedback on task performance.

items.title: Exciting New 'Try It' Prompts for Connectors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces server-driven 'Try It' chat prompts to the connector catalog, enhancing user interaction. Each connector now includes hand-curated suggestions that are pre-localized based on the user's language preferences. The integration simplifies the frontend requirements, allowing for dynamic updates without additional releases.

items.title: Enhance BYOK Credential Scoping for Multi-Site Support in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a significant enhancement to the BYOK credential management system by scoping credentials per site rather than just per user. Key changes include modifications to the persistence layer to accommodate site-specific keys, updates to service methods to handle site origins, and the addition of a migration script to backfill existing records. This ensures that each site maintains its own independent set of credentials, improving security and usability for multi-site tenants.

items.title: Streamline Site Defaults for New Subs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the handling of default values for newly created subsites by extracting the logic into a dedicated utility function. The `apply_site_defaults` function ensures that new subsites are initialized with complete default settings for features, auth, and language at creation time, eliminating the previous delay. Additionally, comprehensive tests have been added to validate the new functionality and ensure existing behavior remains intact.

items.title: Exciting New "Try It" Prompts for Connectors in Frontend Web 🌟
items.summary: This update introduces a hand-curated "Try It" section on each connector's detail pane, enhancing user interaction by providing server-localized chat prompts. The prompts open a new tab in Nexior's chat composer, streamlining the user experience without requiring frontend changes for updates. Key files modified include connection types, rendering logic, and localization support.

items.title: Enhance Chat with Deep-Link Support in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enables the chat feature to read `?query=` parameters from AuthFrontend's connector chips, allowing users to deep-link prompts directly into the composer. The implementation ensures that prompts are pasted without auto-submitting, preserving user experience by allowing edits before sending. Additionally, it safeguards against overwriting existing conversations and maintains local drafts.

items.title: Fix iframe embedding issue for Nexior auth in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN` header from the Nginx configuration, which was blocking iframe embedding for the Nexior authentication process across various platforms. The change ensures that users can successfully log in through the Nexior web and mobile applications without encountering errors. Additional comments have been added to prevent future reintroduction of this header, maintaining security through existing postMessage checks.

items.title: Streamline User Menu by Removing Connections Entry in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update removes the redundant 'Connections' entry from the user-avatar dropdown in the User Center. The functionality remains accessible through the Composer.vue's '+' menu, ensuring a cleaner and more efficient user experience. Key changes include the deletion of the associated dropdown item and its handler, simplifying the user interface.

items.title: Enhance On-Demand MCP Server Tool Loading in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `load_mcp_server` tool, allowing for on-demand loading of MCP server tools, significantly reducing token usage during chat requests. The system now defers loading tools until explicitly requested, improving efficiency and performance. Additionally, a new `<mcp-servers>` block has been added to the system prompt to inform the model of available servers without overwhelming it with unnecessary data.

items.title: Enhance Marketplace Connector Display in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update fixes the display issues for marketplace-installed MCP connectors by ensuring they show the correct icon and localized name instead of a generic fallback. Key changes include modifying the catalog fetching logic and updating how connections are resolved based on their identifiers. As a result, users will now see a more informative and visually appealing representation of their connectors.

items.title: New Research Cycle Insights in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new research report covering 18 candidates, with key findings on holds, skips, and buys. Notable decisions include holding SOL and BONK while skipping several candidates due to liquidity concerns and potential risks. The report ensures a comprehensive overview of the current market landscape.

items.title: Enhance Deploy Process with Immediate Catalog Sync in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces immediate triggering of the skill and connector catalog sync jobs right after each deploy. This ensures that any seed YAML edits are reflected in the database without waiting for the next scheduled CronJob, improving the deployment experience. The changes are safe and idempotent, preventing any potential conflicts during re-deploys.

items.title: Update Google Connector Icon for 2025 in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update replaces the Google connector's catalog icon with the new 2025 gradient 'G' logo, ensuring compliance with branding standards. The previous icon was awkwardly rendered and violated the HTTPS URL requirement. The new icon is now hosted on the AceData CDN and will automatically sync with the database via the existing CronJob.

items.title: Enhance BYOK Test Action for Better UX in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the credentials `test` endpoint by allowing validation of provider, API key, and base URL without requiring a saved credential ID. Users can now test configurations directly, enhancing user experience and reducing clutter in the store. The changes include a new `TestInput` object and updated response handling, along with comprehensive tests for both ID-based and ad-hoc testing flows.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation updates based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include extensive updates across multiple language files, enhancing the localization for various languages such as Arabic, German, and Spanish. This will improve user experience by providing more accurate translations in the Nexior application.

items.title: Enhance legacy task image display in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update fixes the issue where reference images for legacy edit tasks were not displayed in the chat history. By introducing a computed property that prioritizes new fields while falling back to older ones, users can now see the relevant thumbnails immediately. This change ensures compatibility with both new and existing tasks without any regressions.

items.title: Revamp BYOK Test Button for Better User Experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the 'Save and Test Connection' button with a dedicated 'Test' button in the BYOK credential dialog. The new button prevents unintended draft saves during testing and displays the masked API key for better visibility. Additionally, the backend has been updated to support the new payload structure, enhancing the overall functionality.

items.title: Enhance chunk loading and caching strategy in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a robust solution for handling stale chunk errors by modifying the caching strategy for `index.html` and implementing a new error handler. The `nginx.conf` file now prevents caching of the main HTML file, while a new utility listens for chunk load errors and triggers a cache-busted reload. These changes ensure users experience seamless navigation even during deployments.

items.title: Enhance CLI with New Video Parameters in Clis 🎥
items.summary: This update synchronizes the CLI with the latest Kling OpenAPI specifications by adding new parameters for video generation, including camera control and element lists. Additionally, the character orientation option has been updated to enforce an enum, ensuring consistency with the API. These changes enhance functionality and improve user experience when generating videos.

items.title: Updated Kling Docs with Latest Changes in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update syncs the Kling documentation with the latest upstream English guides, incorporating key changes to the video and motion generation API integration guides. Notable modifications include updated column headers and prompt examples to enhance clarity and alignment with the source material. The documentation now reflects the most current model capabilities and usage instructions.

items.title: Enhance Kling Video Tools with New Parameters in M C Ps 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces two new optional parameters, `element_list` and `video_list`, to the Kling video generation tools. The `element_list` allows referencing subjects from the subject library, while `video_list` enables input of reference videos with specific constraints. Additionally, eight unit tests have been added to ensure these parameters are correctly handled.

items.title: Introducing Kling AI Video Generation CLI in Clis 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces the Kling CLI, enabling users to generate videos from text prompts and images using the Kling AI service. Key commands include video generation, motion transfer, and task management, all structured within a standard monorepo format. The CLI supports various models and modes, enhancing flexibility for users in video creation.

items.title: Enhance Kling AI Video Generation API Documentation in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces comprehensive documentation for the Kling AI video generation API, including guides for video generation, motion generation, and task management. Key files added include integration guides and a detailed capability matrix, which consolidates model constraints for easier reference. These enhancements aim to streamline the developer experience and improve API usability.

items.title: Revert image URL persistence changes in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the recent changes made to the image URL persistence functionality in the Platform Service. The modifications removed code that captured reference image inputs for task snapshots, impacting how images are displayed in chat history. This change aims to restore previous behavior and ensure consistency in image handling.

items.title: Enhance Kling API with New Video and Motion Endpoints in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated Kling resource with enhanced capabilities, including a new `/kling/videos` schema and a `/kling/motion` endpoint. The SDK now supports specific Kling parameters through the `Kling` and `AsyncKling` classes, allowing for more tailored video generation and motion transfer functionalities. Key changes include the addition of TypeScript and Python resource files, improving client access to Kling features.

items.title: Enhance Kling with 4K Mode and Model Updates in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `4k` as a valid mode in the Kling OpenAPI spec, enhancing video generation capabilities. Additionally, the model list has been updated to include `kling-v2-6`, `kling-v3`, and `kling-v3-omni`, ensuring users have access to the latest options. Documentation for the new mode and its constraints has also been improved for clarity.

items.title: Enhance Kling Model Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive capability matrix for Kling models, detailing support for parameters like `end_image_url`, `generate_audio`, and `camera_control`. Additionally, descriptions in the API reference have been refined to clarify model and mode constraints, helping users avoid common errors. These changes aim to improve user experience and reduce confusion when switching between models.

items.title: Enhance Kling Capability Matrix with User Confirmation in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive capability matrix for Kling video models, ensuring users are informed before applying unsupported settings. Key changes include the addition of conflict detection and user confirmation prompts in the Model, Mode, and Duration selectors. The enhancements aim to improve user experience by preventing silent failures and providing clear guidance on supported configurations.

items.title: Fix site admin initialization for existing records in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that Nexior correctly initializes existing Site records that have an empty `admins` list when an authenticated user accesses the site. The change modifies the site initialization logic to trigger the backend process that assigns the first authenticated user as an admin, addressing a previously unhandled scenario. Additionally, a patch change file has been added for release tracking.

items.title: Ensure Consistent Site Ownership in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the site creation process to ensure that the authenticated creator is always recorded as the site owner or admin. Key changes include the addition of a helper function to normalize owner fields and the implementation of default values for superuser-created sites. Additionally, existing rows missing critical user information can now be repaired through a dedicated API endpoint.

items.title: Fix image persistence in edit tasks for better chat history in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where reference image thumbnails were not displayed in the chat history for edited tasks. The fix involves modifying the `ImagesEditsHandler.prepare()` method to ensure that `image_urls` and `action` fields are included in the task snapshot. As a result, users will now see the correct reference images and task type labels in their chat history.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from Chinese in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automated translation updates for internationalization (i18n) from the Chinese (zh-CN) source. Key files have been modified across multiple languages, enhancing the localization experience for users. The changes include significant additions and modifications to various language files, improving the overall accessibility of the Nexior platform.

items.title: Fix site persistence issue to prevent flag-flip outages in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the 'site' key from the persisted state in localStorage, addressing a critical issue where users would see outdated feature flags after a site update. By ensuring that the site data is fetched fresh on each page load, we eliminate the risk of users being locked out of new features. The change is seamless for users, requiring no action on their part for the fix to take effect.

items.title: Enhance User Experience with Inline Pills for Inspiration Chips in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces inline removable pills for selected inspiration chips, allowing users to see their choices directly above the prompt textarea. The new component, InspirationPills, ensures a seamless experience by maintaining a single source of truth for selected presets, eliminating the need for state changes. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for the remove button's aria-label, enhancing accessibility.

items.title: Exciting New Veo Endpoints and Features in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces four new Veo endpoints, enhancing user capabilities with actions like upsampling to 4K, video extension, and object manipulation. The ActionSelector now supports eight actions, and several new components have been added for improved user interaction. Additionally, internationalization support has been expanded with new language keys.

items.title: Enhance Admin Permissions Check in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes a critical issue in the admin permissions check that caused a server error when `obj.admins` was None. By implementing a short-circuit for superusers and ensuring a fallback for None values, admin operations can now proceed without encountering a 500 error. This change is essential for maintaining functionality in legacy subsites created under previous configurations.

items.title: Enhance BYOK Experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces three key improvements to the BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) page based on user feedback. The i18n format has been flattened for better compatibility, the Base URL has been moved out of the 'advanced' section for easier access, and the BYOK settings have been relocated to the Settings dialog for a more intuitive user experience. These changes streamline the user interface and enhance usability.

items.title: Exciting Updates: New GLM and WebExtractor Servers with Enhanced VEO Endpoints in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new MCP servers for GLM and WebExtractor, enhancing functionality for chat completions and web rendering. Additionally, VEO has been extended with four new endpoints, allowing for advanced video processing capabilities. Key changes include the addition of new tools and types, ensuring better integration and performance across services.

items.title: Enhance action derivation for video generation in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update fixes the issue where the `end_image_url` was incorrectly rejected during text-to-video requests. The `onGenerate()` method now automatically derives the action based on the provided inputs, ensuring smoother video generation. Additionally, the image upload limit has been corrected to one image, aligning the user experience with the intended functionality.

items.title: Exciting CLI Updates with New Features in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new CLI tools for `glm` and `webextrator`, along with significant enhancements to the `veo` CLI. New endpoints for video manipulation in `veo` include `extend`, `reshoot`, `objects`, and `upscale`. The `glm` CLI now supports chat completions with multiple models, while `webextrator` offers commands for extracting and rendering web content.

items.title: Enhance Documentation for GLM and WebExtrator Services in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces comprehensive documentation for the GLM and WebExtrator services, which were previously missing from the repository. Key additions include detailed guides, API integration instructions, and usage examples for both services, enhancing developer accessibility and understanding. The new documentation aims to streamline the integration process and provide clear insights into the functionalities offered by these APIs.

items.title: Exciting New Resources Added in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new resources for GLM, Veo, and WebExtrator services, enhancing the SDK's capabilities. The GLM service now supports chat completions with multiple model options, while Veo adds several new video operations. Additionally, WebExtrator provides advanced web extraction and rendering features, improving user control over browser interactions.

items.title: Enhance Document Rows and API Mapping for New Endpoints in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces four new `Document` rows for the Veo API endpoints, ensuring they are properly linked and displayed on the platform. Additionally, it refreshes the `service_api_mapping.json` file, which is crucial for the Docs sync pipeline, allowing all six Veo endpoints to be included in the documentation. The changes enhance the API reference and improve user access to the new features.

items.title: Automated Research Cycle Results in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest automated research cycle has been completed, analyzing 14 candidates across various sources. Notably, SOL and BONK have been marked as 'Hold' while 12 candidates were eliminated due to stringent checks. The report highlights key insights and health statuses of the sources used in the analysis.
